what_it_does: This file is an interpretive ledger entry in JSON form. It records a structured reading of the claude-ds project's app/canvases/Bridge.jsx React component, including what the component does, notable observations, standout design points, conventions, concerns, open questions, purpose-vs-actual assessment, themes, intention signals, novelty, contribution, and dense embedding summaries. It contains no code symbols.
observations: The file is a single JSON object with a fixed interpretive schema: project, path, model, what_it_does, observations, standouts, conventions, concerns, notes, questions, purpose_vs_actual, apparent_themes, intention_signals, novelty, contribution, summary_for_embedding, intention_for_embedding.
It documents a React canvas component named Bridge that is assigned to window.Bridge and renders the Environment/Host layer of a design Studio.
All displayed values are described as being read from window.CV_HOST.describe() and window.CV_AI.query({family:'repo'}), not from hand-written status text.
It notes local React hook aliases using a _br suffix (useState_br, etc.) to avoid collisions in a shared global bundle.
It records a concern about literal hex color fallbacks embedded inside var() token references.
The symbols array is explicitly empty because the file contains no code symbols.
standouts: It captures a 'pure projection' design contract: the view recomputes from a host registry rather than maintaining its own status text.
It records a runtime escalation ladder: sandbox review up to browser file access and native/MCP.
It documents a reviewable serialized change queue with copy, apply-to-disk, and reject affordances before disk writes.
conventions: Follows a ledger/interpretive-entry schema with qualitative tag arrays and embedding summaries.
Uses neutral, descriptive language without ranking the described component against others.
Represents second-order readings (themes, intention signals, novelty) as first-class fields.
References external runtime registries (CV_HOST, CV_AI) rather than embedding source code.
concerns: The file asserts 'purpose_vs_actual: Matches' and records 'intention_signals' without showing the underlying source, so the basis for those judgments is not visible in this file alone.
It asks questions about shapes and behaviors (CV_HOST.describe return shape, agent stash lifecycle, __cvFsapiPending writer) that this interpretive file cannot answer.
Some fields like standouts and concerns blur observation with evaluation, which may need explicit confidence/derivation markers in a fused ledger.
notes: An interpretive ledger entry that captures a structured, neutral design reading of the Bridge.jsx canvas component, its registry-projection contract, capability-aware UI, and reviewable change queue.
questions: Where is the underlying Bridge.jsx source file relative to this interpretive JSON?
How are purpose_vs_actual and intention_signals validated against the source?
What consumer uses summary_for_embedding and intention_for_embedding?
What does the 'opus' model field signify for this interpretation?
What is the role of 'wave-002/out' in the build-prep pipeline?
What is the canonical schema version or interpretation timestamp for this ledger entry?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file is a structured interpretive ledger entry for Bridge.jsx and contains the expected descriptive, thematic, and embedding fields.
apparent_themes: Interpretive metadata as a design substrate
Human-in-the-loop change review before disk writes
intention_signals: To produce a machine-readable but qualitative reading of a component that preserves design intent, concerns, and themes so it can be merged with other readings without imposing a single winner.
novelty: A second-order interpretive JSON layer that captures not only factual observations but also themes, inferred intention, novelty, and embedding-ready summaries of a code artifact.
contribution: Contributes a neutral, structured interpretation of the Bridge canvas component—its pure-projection contract, runtime ladder, handoff modes, agent stash, and reviewable serialized-change queue—so a fused system can retain these design insights alongside raw code facts.
summary_for_embedding: An interpretive ledger JSON file documenting the Bridge.jsx React canvas component as a pure projection of CV_HOST and CV_AI registries, with capability-aware runtime connection, handoff mode, and a reviewable serialized change queue.
intention_for_embedding: To capture the design intent and qualitative reading of the Bridge canvas component in a neutral, fusion-ready ledger entry.
